claim: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit detects tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects using git ls-files and filesystem walking fallbacks.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() implementation verified: creates SHA-256 digests using git ls-files for git repos and walkFiles() fallback for non-git, with allowed artifact root exclusion.
evidence.why: Primary implementation evidence supporting multi-mode boundary detection claim.

evidence.summary: Test suite covers git untracked detection (416-452), gitignored file detection (454-491), and non-git fallback mutation detection (493-533), validating all three claimed detection modes.
evidence.why: Comprehensive test evidence confirming detection capabilities across scenarios.

evidence.summary: Demand doctrine confirms boundary audit design intent and enforcement via readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Product documentation validates architectural intent matches implementation.

evidence.summary: Real stress test artifact shows boundary.project_mutation='clean' after full-tool Claude execution, proving detection works with actual agents.
evidence.why: Real-world evidence confirming theoretical implementation works in practice.

evidence.summary: Blocker enforcement code verified: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers force prd_ready=false on violations.
evidence.why: Evidence that detection claims are enforced, not just theoretical.

risks: FileDigest() may fail on large binary files, symlinks, or special filesystem objects not tested in current test coverage.
Transient mutations within execution window (mutate then revert before snapshot) escape detection by design.
Concurrent filesystem changes from IDEs, build tools, or background processes during agent execution could cause false positives.
missing: Evidence for large binary file handling (>100MB) in fileDigest() implementation.
Evidence for symlink and special file handling in boundary snapshot.
Evidence for git submodule handling in gitFiles() implementation.
Performance characteristics for very large repositories (100k+ files) not evidenced.

result.notes: Explorer claim is well-supported by code, tests, docs, and stress test artifacts. Evidence paths are valid and readable. Assumptions are properly separated from facts in explorer result. Missing items are edge cases not blocking core claim. Proceed with treating boundary detection claims as project facts for PRD readiness.
